thesis: The strongest current thesis is the archived 8-layer `SSSL` mixed-window baseline with alternating value embeddings, which reached `val_bpb=0.996233` and remains the best completed evidence on this axis.
antithesis: The strongest current antithesis is the embodied branch baseline in `train.py`: a plain 8-layer `SSSL` stack with `USE_VALUE_EMBEDDINGS = False`, which argues that simpler optimization and fewer parameters may beat the older novelty under the same 5-minute budget.
synthesis_candidate: If the plain baseline is competitive, keep `USE_VALUE_EMBEDDINGS = False`, raise `DEPTH` to 10, and lower `ASPECT_RATIO` to 48 so the removed novelty budget becomes extra ordinary depth instead of coming back as value embeddings.

active_tensions.label: Value embeddings vs simplicity
active_tensions.why_active: The best completed run used alternating value embeddings, while the current branch baseline removes them entirely. This session needs a clean first measurement before deciding whether that roughly 16.8M-parameter novelty was genuinely useful or just short-run clutter.

thesis.summary: Keep alternating value embeddings because the only completed best-in-class run on this axis used them and may have benefited from extra per-token value pathways.

antithesis.summary: Remove value embeddings and trust plain attention plus residual mixing to optimize more cleanly inside the same 300-second budget.

active_tensions.label: Capacity vs throughput
active_tensions.why_active: If the plain stack lands only slightly behind the archived winner, the next question is whether the missing quality came from lost capacity or from losing the specific value-embedding mechanism. That makes a deeper plain follow-up the most natural counterpoint.

thesis.summary: Preserve the plain 8-layer scale first so the branch measures maximal short-run step count with the current simple stack.

antithesis.summary: Spend the saved value-embedding budget on more ordinary depth, even if that costs throughput.

source.text:
# Deeper plain-capacity alternative
DEPTH = 10
ASPECT_RATIO = 48
USE_VALUE_EMBEDDINGS = False

active_tensions.label: Locality vs global context
active_tensions.why_active: The current branch baseline inherits the mixed `SSSL` pattern from the archived winner, but removing value embeddings may change how much the model relies on periodic full-context refresh. This branch still needs to learn whether the mixed pattern is essential or just a throughput convenience.

thesis.summary: Keep the mixed `SSSL` attention pattern because it preserves throughput while still forcing a full-context pass each cycle.

antithesis.summary: Use full-window attention throughout because global coverage may matter more than local efficiency once value embeddings are gone.

active_tensions.label: Aggression vs stability
active_tensions.why_active: The plain baseline keeps the same no-warmup, high-LR sprint schedule, but a simpler model may want a different optimizer temperament than the historical value-embedding winner. This remains unresolved until the branch has one clean unchanged baseline run.

thesis.summary: Keep the aggressive short-horizon optimizer settings because the harness rewards early movement and the first run should avoid extra confounders.

antithesis.summary: Trade some early speed for cleaner convergence with lower Adam rates, a slightly lower Muon LR, and a short warmup.

source.text:
# Conservative schedule alternative
EMBEDDING_LR = 0.45
MATRIX_LR = 0.03
SCALAR_LR = 0.35
WARMUP_RATIO = 0.05
WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.1

transcendent.emergent_thought: If the plain baseline survives but gives back a little quality, the most plausible repair is to turn some of the removed novelty budget into extra ordinary depth rather than restoring the full value-embedding mechanism.
transcendent.concrete_change: Keep `USE_VALUE_EMBEDDINGS = False`, set `DEPTH = 10`, and lower `ASPECT_RATIO` to 48 so the branch tests deeper plain capacity as the third option.
